zh.lifeSummary: Nicholas de Kelly於1097年出生在英國戴文。他和Mrs. Nicholas de Kelly至少有1個兒子。他去世在英國英格蘭戴文。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Nicholas de Kelly于1097年出生在英国戴文。他和Mrs. Nicholas de Kelly至少有1个儿子。他去世在英国英格兰戴文。

nameMeanings.name: Kelly
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Aileen, John Patrick, Siobhan, Liam, Brigid, Donovan, Eamon, Parnell, Aidan, Clancy.

Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Ceallaigh, or, in some areas, Mac Ceallaigh ‘descendant (or son) of Ceallach’, an ancient Irish personal name, originally a byname meaning ‘bright-headed’, later understood as ‘frequenting churches’ (Irish ceall). There are several early Irish saints who bore this name. Kelly is now the most common of all surnames in Ireland.

Scottish: habitational name from either the lands of Kelly, near Arbroath (Angus), Kellie in Carnbee (Fife), or Kelly in Methlick (Aberdeenshire). Each of these placenames is from Pictish celli or Gaelic coille ‘wood’ + the placename forming suffix -in. Alternatively, from the lands of Kelloe in the barony of Hume (Berwickshire), whose name is of uncertain origin.

variants.name: Kelley
meanings: Irish (Offaly) and English: variant of   Kelly  .

variants.name: Kilkelly
meanings: Irish (Galway): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Giolla Ceallaigh ‘son of the devotee of (Saint) Ceallach’ (see Kelly ).

variants.name: Killeen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Donovan, Kieran, Liam.

Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Cillín ‘descendant of Cillín’, a pet form of the personal name Ceallach (see Kelly ). The name was borne by various early Irish saints, including the leader of a 7th-century mission to Franconia and Thuringia, hence the popularity of the personal name Kilian in medieval central Europe.

variants.name: Kellis
meanings: Scottish: patronymic based on   Kelly   as a personal name. Compare   Kellison  .
Greek: descriptive nickname for a bald man, from kelis ‘bald’, a word of Turkish origin (from kel ‘bald’).
History: As a surname of Scottish origin, this has been established in NC since the 18th century.

variants.name: Kellison
meanings: Scottish: patronymic based on   Kelly   as a personal name.

nameMeanings.name: Nicholas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nickolas
meanings: Mainly U.S.: variant spelling of   Nicholas  , influenced by the short form   Nick .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

en.lifeSummary: Nicholas de Kelly was born in 1097, in Devon, England. He had at least 1 son with Mrs. Nicholas de Kelly. He died in Devon, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Nicholas de Kelly est né en 1097, à Devon, Angleterre. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Mrs. Nicholas de Kelly. Il est décédé à Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Nicholas de Kelly wurde 1097 in Devon, England geboren. Er hatte mit Mrs. Nicholas de Kelly mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb in Devon, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Nicholas de Kelly è nato il nel 1097 nel Devon, England. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Mrs. Nicholas de Kelly. Morì nel Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ko.lifeSummary: Nicholas de Kelly(은)는 1097년 영국 데폰에서 태어났다. 그는 Mrs. Nicholas de Kelly(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 영국 잉글랜드 데폰에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Nicholas de Kelly nasceu em 1097, em Devon, Inglaterra. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Mrs. Nicholas de Kelly. Ele faleceu em Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Nicholas de Kelly родился в 1097 г. в местности девон, Англия. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Mrs. Nicholas de Kelly. Он умер в местности девон, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Nicholas de Kelly nació en 1097, en Devon, Inglaterra. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Mrs. Nicholas de Kelly. Murió en Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
